text:
In a sleepy little town called Willow Creek stood Mr. Jenkins’ antique shop. People would stroll past his door wondering what old treasures they might find. One afternoon, a girl named Lily traipsed in, drawn by the smell of dust.

She wandered through shelves lined with dusty photographs and forgotten dolls. Her eyes landed on a peculiar-looking bird feather. Its colors were muted green with silver specks. It looked like a frosty night feather.

"That'll do," Mrs. Jenkins chuckled softly. She handed Lily a little velvet bag.

"You found this?" Mrs. Jenkins eyed the feather closely. "Wow, that must be treasure."

She waved goodbye and skipped home. Tonight was Saturday, perfect for something she loved—a sewing circle.

Inside her cozy room filled with quilt patches, Lily decided to stitch that feather onto her latest quilt. After hours of weaving yarn into stitches, her quilt was softest thing ever. She smiled.

The next Monday, Lily showed off her quilt at school. Friends
